zh.Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reic: 海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇艾爾茲比斯圖姆 麥音
zh.Rieneck, Main-Spessart, Bavaria, Germany: 德國貝也藍德可萊斯 麥-斯培薩特利納可
zh.Burg Rieneck, Rieneck, Landkreis Main-Sp: 德國貝也藍德可萊斯 麥-斯培薩特利納可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz於1175年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇艾爾茲比斯圖姆 麥音時，他的父親Gerard II van Loon29歲，母親Adélaïde von Geldern36歲。他於1204年與Kunigunde von Zimmern結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他在德國海藍-普法爾茲麥音被列為burggraf。他去世於1216年10月24日在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 麥-斯培薩特利納可享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz于1175年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇艾爾茲比斯圖姆 麥音时，他的父亲Gerard II van Loon29岁，母亲Adélaïde von Geldern36岁。他于1204年与Kunigunde von Zimmern结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他在德国海藍-普法爾茲麥音被列为burggraf。他去世于1216年10月24日在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 麥-斯培薩特利納可享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Loon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Perhaps a shortened form of Irish McGoohan, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuacháin or Mag Cuacháin, a patronymic from a byname from cuach ‘cuckoo’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 阮, see   Ruan   3.

variants.name: Ruan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Xiu, Ying, Biao, Chao, Cheng, Guang, Guohua, Hua, Huiming, Jia, Jian Hua.  Spanish Jorge, Arnulfo, Arturo, Blanca, Carlos, Eduardo, Gregorio, Guillermo, Javier, Jesus, Jose, Josefina.
Irish and Scottish: variant of   Ruane  .
Spanish (Ruán): nickname from ruán ‘roan’ (a color of horses), a variant of Ruano .

nameMeanings.name: Rieneck
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German:
variant of   Reinecke  .
habitational name from any of the places in Germany and Switzerland called Reineck or Rheineck, for example one in Bavaria and another near Rorschach, Switzerland.

variants.name: Reinecke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gunther, Hans, Manfred, Otto, Wilhelm.
North German: from a pet form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names formed with ragin ‘counsel’ as the first element. See also Reincke .

variants.name: Reinecker
meanings: German: habitational name for someone from any of various places called Reineck or Rheineck (see   Reineck  ).

nameMeanings.name: Mainz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Math, Otto, Siegbert, Walther.
German and Jewish (western Ashkenazic): habitational name from the city of Mainz in Germany.

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’. In the later Middle Ages this was a more common name than Gerald , with which it was sometimes confused. Nowadays it is less common, surviving mainly among Roman Catholics, in honour of the many saints of this name.

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: Variant spelling of   Gerard  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Jerrard
meanings: Rare variant spelling of   Gerard  , probably influenced by the form of the modern surname, if not a transferred use.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz was born in 1175, in Erzbistum Mainz, Holy Roman Empire, his father, Gerard II van Loon, was 29 and his mother, Adélaïde von Geldern, was 36. He married Kunigunde von Zimmern in 1204. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. His occupation is listed as burggraf in Mainz, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. He died on 24 October 1216, in Rieneck, Main-Spessart, Bavaria, Germany, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz est né en 1175, à Erzbistum Mainz, Saint-Empire romain, son père, Gerard II van Loon, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Adélaïde von Geldern, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Kunigunde von Zimmern en 1204. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est burggraf à Mainz, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne. Il est décédé le 24 octobre 1216, à Rieneck, Landkreis Main-Spessart, Bayern, Allemagne, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz 1175 in Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gerard II van Loon 29 und seine Mutter Adélaïde von Geldern 36. Er hat 1204 Kunigunde von Zimmern geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als burggraf in Mainz, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. Oktober 1216 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Rieneck, Landkreis Main-Spessart, Bayern, Deutschland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz è nato il nel 1175 nel Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich, il suo padre, Gerard II van Loon, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Adélaïde von Geldern, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Kunigunde von Zimmern nel 1204. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come burggraf nel Mainz, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania. Morì il 24 ottobre 1216, nel Rieneck, Landkreis Main-Spessart, Bayern, Germania, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainzが1175年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチエルズビストゥム メインズで生まれた時，父Gerard II van Loonは29歳，母Adélaïde von Geldernは36歳であった。1204年にKunigunde von Zimmernと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。ドイツルヘインランド-プファルズメインズでの職業はburggrafと記録されている。彼は1216年10月24日にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス メイン-スペッサルトリーンエックで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz(이)가 1175년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 르즈비스투 맹츠에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerard II van Loon(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Adélaïde von Geldern(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1204년 Kunigunde von Zimmern(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 맹츠에서 burggraf이었다. 그는 1216년 10월 24일 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 맹-스페사르트 리니크에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz nasceu em 1175, em Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich, seu pai, Gerard II van Loon, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Adélaïde von Geldern, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Kunigunde von Zimmern em 1204. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como burggraf em Mainz, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha. Ele faleceu em 24 de outubro de 1216, em Rieneck, Landkreis Main-Spessart, Bayern, Alemanha, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz родился в 1175 г. в местности эрзбистум меинз, хилигесь рoмищесь раич, его отцу по имени Gerard II van Loon было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Adélaïde von Geldern, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Kunigunde von Zimmern в 1204 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери.  Он работал по профессии burggraf в местности меинз, раинланд-пфалз, Германия. Он умер 24 октября 1216 г. в местности риинекк, ландкрис меин-спессарт, беиирн, Германия в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz nació en 1175, en Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich, su papá, Gerard II van Loon, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Adélaïde von Geldern, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Kunigunde von Zimmern en 1204. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era burggraf en Mainz, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania. Murió el 24 de octubre de 1216, en Rieneck, Landkreis Main-Spessart, Bayern, Alemania, a la edad de 41 años.
